name: binwalk
title: binwalk
summary: tool library for analyzing binary blobs and executable code
description:
tool library for analyzing binary blobs and executable code
Binwalk is a tool for searching a given binary image for embedded files
and executable code. Specifically, it is designed for identifying files
and code embedded inside of firmware images. Binwalk uses the libmagic
library, so it is compatible with magic signatures created for the Unix
file utility.
.
Binwalk also includes a custom magic signature file which contains
improved signatures for files that are commonly found in firmware images
such as compressed/archived files, firmware headers, Linux kernels,
bootloaders, filesystems, etc.
.
This package is an empty package, because the binary tool is already
provided with the library, dependency of this package.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use binwalk because tool library for analyzing binary blobs and executable code.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use binwalk as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `binwalk [OPTIONS] [FILE1] [FILE2] [FILE3] ...` as documented upstream for binwalk.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair binwalk with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `binwalk --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: binwalk - tool for searching binary images for embedded files and executable code
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install binwalk. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `binwalk [OPTIONS] [FILE1] [FILE2] [FILE3] ...`. tool library for analyzing binary blobs and executable code.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: tool library for analyzing binary blobs and executable code.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: binwalk - tool for searching binary images for embedded files and executable code.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install binwalk
Install with: sudo apt install binwalk
